Package org.eventb.core.seqprover.tactics.tests

Class NNFRewritesAutoTacTests

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.core.seqprover.tactics.tests.AbstractTacticTests
org.eventb.core.seqprover.tactics.tests.NNFRewritesAutoTacTests
public class NNFRewritesAutoTacTests extends AbstractTacticTests

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    applyHybrid()
    Ensures that the NnfRewrites auto-tactic succeeds on hybrid configurations : When there are two predicate in the hypothesis (impossible for the goal) When there are one predicate in the hypothesis and one in the goal
    void
    applyMany()
    Ensures that the NnfRewrites auto-tactic succeeds many times on both hypothesis and goal in those configuration : recursively on one predicate on one predicate containing two sub-predicates on which the tactic can be applied
    void
    applyOnce()
    Ensures that the NnfRewrites auto-tactic succeeds once on both Hypothesis and Goal which contains : a disjunction a conjunction an implication an existential quantifier a universal quantifier a double negation
    void
    assertFailureInHypAndGoal(String predStr)
     
    void
    assertSuccessInHypAndGoal(String predStr)
     
    void
    failOnce()
    Ensures that the NnfRewrites auto-tactic fails on both Hypothesis and Goal which contains : predicate which contains a rule that is implemented in the underlying reasoner but shall not be used (¬S=∅, 1=1⇔2=2) no ¬ (e.g.

    Methods inherited from class org.eventb.core.seqprover.tactics.tests.AbstractTacticTests

    addToTypeEnvironment, assertFailure, assertSuccess, genProofTreeNode, parseExpression, parsePredicate, setFormulaFactory, tacticRegistered

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

  • Constructor Details

    • NNFRewritesAutoTacTests

      public NNFRewritesAutoTacTests()

  • Method Details

    • applyOnce

      public void applyOnce()
      Ensures that the NnfRewrites auto-tactic succeeds once on both Hypothesis and Goal which contains :
      • a disjunction
      • a conjunction
      • an implication
      • an existential quantifier
      • a universal quantifier
      • a double negation

    • applyMany

      public void applyMany()
      Ensures that the NnfRewrites auto-tactic succeeds many times on both hypothesis and goal in those configuration :
      • recursively on one predicate
      • on one predicate containing two sub-predicates on which the tactic can be applied

    • applyHybrid

      public void applyHybrid()
      Ensures that the NnfRewrites auto-tactic succeeds on hybrid configurations :
      • When there are two predicate in the hypothesis (impossible for the goal)
      • When there are one predicate in the hypothesis and one in the goal

    • failOnce

      public void failOnce()
      Ensures that the NnfRewrites auto-tactic fails on both Hypothesis and Goal which contains :
      • predicate which contains a rule that is implemented in the underlying reasoner but shall not be used (¬S=∅, 1=1⇔2=2)
      • no ¬ (e.g. 1=1 ∧ 2=2)
      • a ¬ that cannot be removed or pushed in its children (e.g. ¬1=1)

    • assertSuccessInHypAndGoal

      public void assertSuccessInHypAndGoal(String predStr)

    • assertFailureInHypAndGoal

      public void assertFailureInHypAndGoal(String predStr)
